enrichment.seo_meta_description: debugmail.io is a fake SMTP server for testing outgoing emails in development. See its pricing plans, features, and how it compares for developers.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: debugmail.io is a fake SMTP server and email testing tool built to solve a common development problem: safely capturing and inspecting outgoing emails from an application without those emails actually being delivered to real users. Instead of connecting an application's mail-sending code to a live SMTP provider during development or staging, developers point it at debugmail.io's SMTP endpoint, and every email the application tries to send is intercepted, stored, and made viewable inside a web-based mailbox rather than being delivered externally. This lets engineers verify that transactional emails, such as password resets, order confirmations, or notification digests, render correctly and contain the expected content and links, without risking accidentally spamming real customers, cluttering personal inboxes, or triggering unwanted side effects in test environments. Captured emails can be viewed in both HTML and plain-text formats directly in the browser, downloaded as standard .EML files for use in other email clients or for archival, and, when needed, manually forwarded to a real email address for a final visual check before a feature ships. The platform is organized around projects and shareable mailboxes, letting a team assign a project to a specific application or environment, share access with teammates, and generate public links to individual captured emails for quick review or bug reports, which supports collaborative QA workflows where a developer, a designer, and a QA engineer might all need to inspect the same test email. debugmail.io publishes a tiered pricing structure of Free, Gold, and Premium plans. The Free plan includes one project, two team members, unlimited test emails per day, one forwarded email per day, and 10MB of mailbox storage, and the site notes a two-week trial period after which usage converts to a rate of about 2 US dollars per user per month, an important nuance for teams assuming the Free plan is a permanent zero-cost tier. The Gold plan, at about 5 US dollars per user per month, raises limits to unlimited projects, twenty team members, unlimited test emails per day, one hundred forwarded emails per day, 1GB of storage, and one forwarding rule per project. The Premium plan removes remaining caps entirely, offering unlimited projects and team members, unlimited emails and forwarding, 5GB of storage, and twenty forwarding rules per project, though its exact price is not listed publicly and likely requires contacting sales. Notably, debugmail.io also offers a free Gold-tier plan specifically for open-source projects, extending fuller functionality to OSS maintainers at no cost. The service is operated by WBTech, and the company does not publish a founding year or detailed headquarters information on its main site, though third-party company databases associate it with Moscow, Russia; this detail should be treated as less authoritative than information sourced directly from the company. debugmail.io markets itself with logos suggesting it is used by or trusted in environments similar to those of major organizations such as Microsoft, Disney, Oracle, IBM, and SAP, and the roadmap referenced on its site includes upcoming IMAP integration, spam-score rating, and a dedicated mail-client-style viewer, though these should be understood as planned rather than guaranteed features. As a directory entry in 2026, debugmail.io fits into the broader category of fake SMTP or email-sandbox testing tools alongside competitors such as Mailtrap and MailHog, differentiated primarily by its project-and-mailbox sharing model, its free tier for open-source projects, and its per-user rather than purely per-project pricing structure.

long_form_blocks.heading: What debugmail.io Does
paragraphs: debugmail.io acts as a fake SMTP server that intercepts outgoing emails from an application in development or staging environments, capturing them in a web-based mailbox instead of delivering them to real recipients.
This lets developers and QA teams verify email content, formatting, and links safely, without the risk of sending test emails to real customers or cluttering personal inboxes.

long_form_blocks.heading: Team and Project Features
paragraphs: Emails are organized by project, and mailboxes can be shared with teammates, with public links available for individual captured emails to support quick review and bug reporting.
Captured emails can be viewed in HTML or plain text, downloaded as .EML files, and optionally forwarded to a real email address for a final check before release.

long_form_blocks.heading: Free, Gold, and Premium Pricing
paragraphs: The Free plan includes 1 project, 2 team members, unlimited test emails per day, and 10MB of storage, though the site notes a two-week trial period after which usage is billed at about 2 US dollars per user per month.

The Gold plan (about 5 US dollars per user per month) raises limits to 20 team members and unlimited projects, while the Premium plan removes remaining caps on projects, team members, emails, and forwarding rules; a free Gold-tier plan is also available specifically for open-source projects.

features.title: Fake SMTP server
features.description: Intercepts outgoing application emails during development so they never reach real inboxes.

features.title: HTML and plain-text preview
features.description: View captured emails in either rendered HTML or raw plain-text format directly in the browser.

features.title: Downloadable .EML files
features.description: Export captured emails as standard .EML files for use in other clients or for archiving.

features.title: Email forwarding
features.description: Manually forward a captured test email to a real address for a final visual check.

features.title: Shared team mailboxes
features.description: Organize captured emails by project and share mailbox access with teammates.

features.title: Public share links
features.description: Generate a public link to an individual captured email for quick review or bug reports.

features.title: Free plan for open-source projects
features.description: Offers Gold-tier functionality at no cost specifically for open-source project maintainers.

features.title: Team collaboration tools
features.description: Supports multiple team members per project with role-based access across plans.

pros: Prevents test emails from accidentally reaching real users during development
Supports both HTML and plain-text preview plus .EML export for thorough email QA
Shared mailboxes and public links make collaborative email review straightforward
Free tier is available for open-source projects with fuller functionality than the standard free plan
Straightforward SMTP-based integration that works with most application mail-sending code
Per-user pricing on paid plans is relatively low compared to many developer-tool subscriptions
cons: The Free plan converts to a paid rate after a two-week trial period, which may surprise users expecting a permanently free tier
Company founding year, exact headquarters, and team size are not clearly published on the official site
Premium plan pricing is not publicly listed and likely requires contacting sales
Storage limits on Free and Gold plans (10MB and 1GB) may be restrictive for high-volume testing
Roadmap items like IMAP integration and spam-score rating were described as upcoming rather than currently available
Less brand recognition and community documentation compared to larger competitors like Mailtrap

faqs.question: What is debugmail.io used for
faqs.answer: It is used as a fake SMTP server to capture and inspect outgoing emails from an application during development, so test emails never reach real inboxes.

faqs.question: Is debugmail.io free
faqs.answer: There is a free plan with 1 project and 2 team members, though the site notes a two-week trial period after which usage converts to a paid rate of about 2 US dollars per user per month; a free Gold-tier plan is also offered for open-source projects.

faqs.question: How much do paid debugmail.io plans cost
faqs.answer: The Gold plan costs about 5 US dollars per user per month, while Premium plan pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting the company.

faqs.question: Can I forward test emails to a real inbox
faqs.answer: Yes, debugmail.io supports forwarding captured test emails to a real email address for a final review, with daily forwarding limits that vary by plan.

faqs.question: Does debugmail.io support team collaboration
faqs.answer: Yes, projects and mailboxes can be shared among team members, and individual captured emails can be shared via public links.

faqs.question: Can I download captured emails from debugmail.io
faqs.answer: Yes, captured emails can be downloaded as standard .EML files for use in other email clients or for archiving.

faqs.question: Who operates debugmail.io
faqs.answer: The service is operated by WBTech; the company does not publish a founding year or detailed headquarters information on its official site.

faqs.question: What features are planned for debugmail.io
faqs.answer: The company has referenced upcoming IMAP integration, spam-score rating, and a dedicated mail-client-style viewer as planned additions.

use_cases.title: Testing transactional email templates
use_cases.description: Verifies that password resets, order confirmations, and other transactional emails render correctly before shipping to production.

use_cases.title: QA review of email content and formatting
use_cases.description: Lets QA teams inspect captured HTML and plain-text emails for bugs without needing access to real user inboxes.

use_cases.title: Safe staging environment email testing
use_cases.description: Prevents staging or QA environments from accidentally sending real emails to customers by capturing all outgoing mail.

use_cases.title: Open-source project email testing
use_cases.description: Provides open-source maintainers a free way to test email-sending features in their projects without cost.

pricing_plans.price: Free (converts to paid rate after 2-week trial)
